summary: None of the options provided—niacin, pantothenic acid, cyanocobalamin, or folic acid—are fully synthesized in the human body; all require at least partial external supplementation from food sources due to their roles as essential nutrients critical for various physiological processes like metabolism regulation and DNA synthesis support among other functions vital across multiple organ systems including but not limited to brain health maintenance via neurotransmitter production mechanisms dependent upon adequate levels maintained throughout life stages ranging from infancy up until
